verses:
nāce śacī⋅nandana, bhakatajīvana dhana,
saṅge nāce priya nityānanda
advaita śrīnivāsa, āra nāce haridāsa,
vāsughoṣa rāya rāmānanda

nityānanda mukha heri, bole pa̐hu hari hari,
premāya dharaṇī gaḍi jāya
priya gadādhara āsi, prabhura bāma pāśe basi,
ghana naraharimukha cāya

pa̐hu nāhi mele ā̐khi, kahe mora kāhā̐ sakhī,
kāhā̐ pāba rāi daraśana
kaha kaha narahari, āra sambarite nāri,
ihā bali bhela acetana

ekhani āchalu̐ tathā, ke more ānila ethā,
rāsa⋅rase nikuñja⋅bhavana
gela sukhasampada, ebe bhela vipada,
viṣādaye e dāsa locana
en_translation:
Śacī⋅nandana, the treasure and life of the devotees, dances. With Him dances dear Nityānanda. Advaita, Śrīnivāsa, and Haridāsa also dance. Vāsughoṣa and Rāya Rāmānanda likewise dance.

Seeing Nityānanda's face, the Lord chants "Hari! Hari!" and rolls on the earth in divine love. Dear Gadādhara comes and sits on the Lord's left side, frequently looking at Narahari's face.

The Lord does not open His eyes, saying, "Where is my sakhī? Where will I get the audience of Rādhā?" "Tell me, tell me, Narahari, I can no longer control myself," Saying this, He became unconscious.

"Just now I was there, who brought me here? In the forest grove with the mellows of the Rāsa dance." All happiness and opulence is gone, now there is only calamity— thus the servant Locana Dāsa laments.
